title: subagent: BACKGROUND_AGENTS and TRANSCRIPT_STORE globals defeat testability and isolation
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/subagent.rs:365-366 (BACKGROUND_AGENTS) and src/subagent.rs:381-382 (TRANSCRIPT_STORE)
## Violation
```rust
pub static BACKGROUND_AGENTS: LazyLock<BackgroundAgentManager> =
    LazyLock::new(BackgroundAgentManager::new);
```

```rust
pub(crate) static TRANSCRIPT_STORE: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<String, StoredTranscript>>> =
    LazyLock::new(|| Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
```

Both are process-global mutable singletons. Consequences:

1. Two tests that register agents share state and pollute each other (the test suite already works around this with random UUIDs).
2. Coordinator agents and the top-level CLI agent share one BackgroundAgentManager — there is no per-leader isolation, breaking the "always-allow per teammate" guarantee the LeaderPermissionBridge is designed to enforce.
3. Dependency Inversion violation: every caller depends on a concrete global instead of a `Arc<dyn AgentRegistry>` trait object.
4. The transcripts global holds raw `Vec<Value>` cloning the full message history on every load — no test can swap it for an in-memory mock.

This is the classic Singleton anti-pattern (heuristic 3.1, "Reject God Objects … force division into granular services").

## Mandated refactor
Define a `trait BackgroundAgentRegistry: Send + Sync` and a `trait TranscriptStore: Send + Sync`. Make `run_subagent` take `&dyn BackgroundAgentRegistry, &dyn TranscriptStore` (or an `AppHandles` bundle). Keep a single shared instance constructed in `main.rs` and inject it. Remove the globals or, if retained, mark them `#[cfg(not(test))]` and provide test-only constructor.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
